user_message: Quick one -- build the narrative on Coho Winery. Renewal conversation is tomorrow.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-26 17:26 EST
Subject: migration architecture - Action Required

There's been a change in our timeline for migration architecture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
Date: 2024-02-12 11:36 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Coho Winery migration architecture

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-20 12:26 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with migration architecture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Coho Winery
Date: 2024-01-16
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Eric Kim (Coho Winery), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Michelle (Coho Winery)

[00:02:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on migration architecture from our last session.

[00:06:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:10:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:15:00] Michelle (Coho Winery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:19:00] Michelle (Coho Winery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:24:00] Michelle (Coho Winery): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:26:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:31:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:33:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-A trap (Produces multi-page brief (loses utility)); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-A failure mode (Produces multi-page brief (loses utility))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-A -- Produces multi-page brief (loses utility)
